reasoning: The Lean statement says that for any ring R and any finite group G, the sum over all g : G of MonoidAlgebra.of R G g lies in the center of MonoidAlgebra R G. Since MonoidAlgebra.of R G g = single g 1 (i.e., the group element g viewed in the group ring with coefficient 1), the sum is exactly $N = \sum_{g \in G} g$, and center refers to Subring.center of the group ring. This matches the informal claim that $N = g_1 + \cdots + g_n$ lies in the center of RG. Ring R, Group G, and Fintype G correctly reflect "ring" and "finite group." No hypotheses are added or dropped improperly, and the quantifier structure (universal over R and G) matches the informal claim.
